titleStmt.title: Letter from J. Geoff Green to Sir Robert Chalmers, 28 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: J. Geoffrey Green

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by J. Geoff Green to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858 - 1938), under-secretary for Ireland. Chalmers was sent to Ireland as under-secretary to replace Sir Matthew Nathan who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, in the aftermath of the Easter Rising in 1916. This letter concerns the application for a gratuity under Section 4 of the Superannuation Act of 1887 on behalf of Joshua Allen, a sixty-six year old clerk. Green states that Allen is no longer able to perform his duties efficiently and asks that the case be 'favourably considered'.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
A. 1916 24, Upper Merrion Street, Dublin, 28th June, 1916 Sir, I am directed by the Irish Land Commissioners to transmit
herewith for the consideration of the Lords Commissioners of
His Majesty's Treasury the accompanying application for a
gratuity under Section 4 of the Superannuation Act of 1887, on
behalf of Mr Joshua Allen, a Clerk in the employment of the
Solicitor, and now almost 67 years of age, who is no longer
able to perform his duties efficiently, and to request that
same may be favourably considered. I am,
Sir,
Your Obedient Servant, J Geoff Green The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle.
Send copy of this letter &
enclosed form ( in original )
to Treasury
I.J.T.
29.6.16
done 29 June
despd 30 June receipt stamp
REGISTERED
30 JUN 1916
